mcqs.question: In a zero-indexed array, the first element is stored at which index?
mcqs.explanation: In zero-indexed arrays (used in C, C++, Java, Python), the first element is stored at index 0. This is because the address formula uses multiplication by index, making 0 the logical starting point.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of linear search in an array of n elements?
mcqs.explanation: Linear search checks each element sequentially until finding the target. In the worst case, the target is at the last position or not present, requiring n comparisons.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid array operation?
mcqs.explanation: Merging is typically an operation performed on two separate data structures (like merging two sorted arrays), not a single array operation. The basic array operations are traversal, insertion, deletion, searching, and sorting.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at a specific position in the middle of an array?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion in the middle requires shifting all elements from the insertion point to the end one position to the right, which takes O(n) time in the worst case.

mcqs.question: In a row-major representation of a 2D array, how is element A[i][j] accessed?
mcqs.explanation: In row-major order, elements are stored row by row. The address formula is Base + ((i * number_of_columns) + j) * element_size.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm has the best time complexity for already sorted arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion Sort has O(n) time complexity for already sorted arrays because no swaps are needed - each element is just compared and found to be in correct position.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of arrays for dynamic data?
mcqs.explanation: Traditional arrays have a fixed size determined at declaration. For dynamic data where the number of elements changes, this is a significant limitation.

mcqs.question: How many comparisons are needed in the worst case for binary search on 64 elements?
mcqs.explanation: Binary search reduces the search space by half each time. log₂(64) = 6, so at most 6 comparisons are needed.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is true about 2D arrays in C programming language?

options.text: They are stored in column-major order
They are stored in row-major order

mcqs.explanation: C uses row-major order for storing 2D arrays in memory, meaning elements are stored row by row contiguously.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of creating a copy of an array of size n?
mcqs.explanation: Creating a copy requires storing n elements, so the space complexity is O(n).

mcqs.question: Which operation has the same time complexity in both arrays and linked lists?

options.text: Deleting at end (if tail pointer available)

mcqs.explanation: Both arrays and linked lists have O(n) time complexity for linear search, as both require traversing elements sequentially.

mcqs.question: What happens when you try to insert an element into a full static array?

options.text: The insertion fails or causes overflow error

mcqs.explanation: Static arrays have fixed size. When full, any insertion attempt causes an overflow condition or the insertion fails.

mcqs.question: In which scenario would you prefer an array over a linked list?

options.text: When frequent insertions/deletions are needed at the beginning
When the size is unknown and changes frequently
When random access by index is the primary operation

mcqs.explanation: Arrays provide O(1) random access by index, while linked lists require O(n) for the same operation. Arrays are preferred when random access is frequent.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of deleting the last element from an array?
mcqs.explanation: Deleting the last element requires no shifting of other elements, so it takes O(1) constant time.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum number of comparisons required for binary search on a sorted array of 100 elements?
mcqs.explanation: log₂(100) ≈ 6.64, which rounds up to 7. So maximum 7 comparisons are needed.

mcqs.question: Which of the following statements about arrays is FALSE?

options.text: Array elements are stored in contiguous memory locations
Arrays allow random access to any element
Arrays have fixed size in most programming languages
Arrays always use more memory than linked lists for the same number of elements

mcqs.explanation: Arrays typically use less memory than linked lists because linked lists need additional memory for pointers/references. Arrays only store the data elements.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity to find the minimum element in an unsorted array?
mcqs.explanation: Finding minimum requires traversing all elements once, which takes O(n) time. There is no faster way without pre-processing.

mcqs.question: In a 3x4 matrix (3 rows, 4 columns) stored in row-major order, what is the index of element A[2][3] in the linear array?
mcqs.explanation: Row-major index = (2 * 4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11. Note: Using 0-based indexing, row 2 is the third row and column 3 is the fourth column.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is the correct order of time complexities for array operations from best to worst for insertion?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion at end is O(1), at beginning is O(n), and at middle is O(n). So from best to worst: End, Middle, Beginning (both middle and beginning are O(n) but beginning requires shifting all n elements).

mcqs.question: What is the primary reason for using 0-based indexing in most programming languages?
mcqs.explanation: It simplifies memory address calculation. The address of element at index i is simply Base + (i * size), making the formula cleaner than 1-based indexing.

options.text: It was the first indexing system invented
